title: A Super-Fast Light Detector That Works Like Magic
summary: Scientists at Duke University made a new device that can see light super fast, in just 125 picoseconds, which is way quicker than a blink. This device is called a photodetector, and it works by feeling the tiny heat from light. It can see all colors of light, even ones our eyes can't see, and it doesn't need batteries or to be cold. It's very thin and can be put on tiny chips. This could help make better cameras for finding skin cancer, checking food, and helping farmers grow crops. The device uses special tiny cubes made of silver to trap light energy, making it work quickly and efficiently. This is a big step because older detectors were slow and bulky. The team shared their work in a science journal.

a very short time, like a trillionth of a second

background_read: Light is a type of energy we can see, but there are many colors like infrared and ultraviolet that our eyes can't see. Photodetectors are tools that help scientists and cameras detect light by turning it into signals. Heat is a form of energy that things give off when they warm up. At Duke University, engineers work on making new gadgets to solve problems. This new device is special because it's fast and thin, unlike old ones that were slow and big. It could make cameras better for health and farming.
Article_Structure: WHAT: Made a fast photodetector that detects light by heat
WHERE: At Duke University in a lab
WHY: To help with things like skin cancer detection and farming

perspectives.description: Scientists built a new device to improve technology for detecting light.
